nominations.title: On multiply-imputed data, common model-selection and likelihood-ratio statistics carry a systematic bias that favors candidate models with more missing information, and the bias is removed by adding a single computable trace term per model.
nominations.reason: The headline takeaway for a skimming practitioner; backed by C1's firm +½·tr(RIV) result and C3's model-specific tilt with the tr(RIV_k) fix.

nominations.title: The averaged log-likelihood across imputations overstates its complete-data counterpart by one half the trace of the relative-increase-in-variance matrix plus a design-imbalance term that vanishes exactly when data are missing completely at random, provided the model has an estimated scale.
nominations.reason: The core analytic result, proved and independently verified in two computer-algebra systems and Monte Carlo (W1); scope-limited to firm trace term plus order-one MAR term as the brief permits.

nominations.title: For information-criterion selection there is no protective reference distribution, so the per-model correction tr(RIV_k) must be applied to restore complete-data-equivalent ranking; for a calibrated likelihood-ratio test (Chan 2022) the mean is already absorbed and the correction must not be applied a second time.
nominations.reason: Tells practitioners exactly where to act and where double-correction inflates size (W2: 0.042 vs over-corrected 0.034); high decision value on a single read.

nominations.title: We demonstrate a structured, ORCID-verified AI-human workflow whose every load-bearing claim passes derive/dual-CAS/Monte-Carlo gates with preregistration and cross-model adversarial review, and show this discipline catches errors single-lineage derivation would ship, such as eight of nine blind re-derivations returning the opposite sign of the main theorem.
nominations.reason: First-class because its evidence (58 verified episodes, the E14 sign-flip catch) is mechanically verified and the panel's open question resolves toward headline; ranked 4 so it leads the technical results without displacing the applied takeaways a skimmer needs most.

nominations.title: In nested likelihood-ratio comparison the differential deviance bias at the null is the missing information of the tested directions projected in the complete-data information metric, which is strictly smaller than the naive difference of per-model trace corrections.
nominations.reason: Firm, proved and verified, with Monte Carlo separating exact from naive at 52 standard errors; reframed to 'at the null' to match the brief's scope.

nominations.title: Uncorrected MI-AIC and MI-BIC systematically tilt toward the most-missing-information candidate, supplying the theoretical justification left open by the prior MI-AIC proposal and recovering Shimodaira's covariate-shift penalty.
nominations.reason: Fills the IC category with a firm directional result plus the measured preregistered prediction (100% of misclassifications on largest-RIV); ranked below the LRT result as more specialized.

nominations.title: We disclose two structural limits of the correction: the equal-fractions candidate family hides the over-statement of the naive trace difference, and per-model marginal transforms cannot calibrate a difference distribution.
nominations.reason: Proved limits that keep practitioners from over-generalizing the fix; earns a slot as an honest-scope implication backed by C3's two disclosed limits.

nominations.title: The bias is firm only with an estimated scale and collapses to zero or minus one half the trace under known scale, so the correction should be applied only to estimated-scale fits.
nominations.reason: A scoping caveat the brief explicitly supports (C1 known-scale collapse) that prevents misapplication; a practical guardrail for skimmers.

bench: Sharing imputations across nested fits collapses paired Monte-Carlo noise from order root-n to order one (C2 efficiency claim)
Convention-free conditional-entropy reading cross-confirming the bias (C1 alternate derivation)
Honest-limit statement that session-coding frequencies are same-lineage model judgments while quotes/artifacts/arithmetic are verified (C4 caveat)
Calibration ladder locates the null/noncentral boundary for corrected IC selection (C3 detail)
gaps: Eight-item cap forces LRT and IC each into a single application sentence and crowds the efficiency/shared-imputation result onto the bench.
